The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in soothing digestive issues and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Galium crissum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Galium crissum is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
